note: Case contract for the radiology module. annotations.x/y are percentages (0-100) of the image. tone: opaque | lucent | finding. level gates visibility by difficulty (beginner shows beginner; intermediate adds intermediate; advanced shows all). Drop licensed dataset images into cases/<type>/ and add entries here.

cases.title: Panoramic, adult full dentition
cases.description: A real panoramic radiograph. Work left to right: condyles, sinuses, then the dentition and mandibular borders. Use the measurement tool to assess symmetry and bone height.
keyFindings: Identify the mandibular condyles and rami on both sides
Locate the maxillary sinuses above the posterior teeth
Trace the inferior alveolar canal through each side of the mandible
Check for third molars and their angulation

annotations.note: Rounded head of the mandible in the joint. Compare both sides for symmetry.
Air-filled radiolucency above the upper molars. A normal landmark, not a lesion.
Paired sinus on the opposite side; the floor often dips between roots.
Radiolucent band carrying the nerve through the mandible. Map it before lower-molar surgery.
Vertical bony plate; assess width and any radiolucent or radiopaque lesions.

cases.title: Periapical, mandibular molars
cases.description: A real periapical of lower molars. Read each tooth crown to apex: enamel, dentin, pulp, then the periapical bone and the crestal bone between teeth.
keyFindings: Assess each interproximal surface for caries below the contact point
Identify any existing restorations and their margins
Check the pulp chambers and root canals
Evaluate the periapical bone and the alveolar crest height

annotations.note: Bright outer crown of the molar. Scan the contact points for dark carious notches.
Dark central space; note its size relative to caries or restorations.
Where the molar roots divide. Radiolucency here can mean furcation bone loss.
Bone level between teeth. Compare against the cemento-enamel junctions.
Check each apex for a radiolucency or widened PDL suggesting apical pathology.

cases.title: Panoramic survey, mixed findings
cases.description: Full-mouth panoramic. Survey the dentition, sinuses, and mandibular borders, then localise the focal findings.
keyFindings: Impacted lower third molar, mesioangular, at the right posterior
Periapical radiolucency at an upper left molar apex
Interproximal caries on an upper anterior tooth
Maxillary sinuses and inferior alveolar canal are normal landmarks, not pathology

annotations.note: Normal air-filled radiolucency above the upper molars. Know it so you do not mistake it for a lesion.
Paired with the left sinus. The floor often dips between molar roots.
Faint radiolucent band running through the mandible. Trace it before any lower molar surgery.
Radiolucent notch at a contact point. Compare the same surface on neighbouring teeth.
Rounded dark zone at a root apex with loss of lamina dura. Suggests apical periodontitis.
Angled, unerupted molar lodged against the tooth in front. Note its relationship to the canal.

cases.title: Periapical, apical radiolucency
cases.description: Single-tooth periapical. Read crown to apex and judge the periapical bone.
keyFindings: Periapical radiolucency at the root apex with loss of lamina dura
Interproximal caries on the crown
Pulp chamber and root canal are patent

annotations.note: Brightest tissue. The dense outer shell of the crown.
Dark central space. Watch its width and continuity to the apex.
Bone height beside the tooth. Drops in periodontal disease.
Thin dark line along the root. Uniform width is healthy.
Radiolucent notch below the contact point.
Dark halo at the apex with lost lamina dura. The headline finding here.

cases.title: Periapical, sound apex
cases.description: A near-normal periapical for orientation. Identify the structures and the single carious surface.
keyFindings: Interproximal caries on the crown
Intact lamina dura and normal periapical bone
No periapical radiolucency

annotations.note: The bright crown shell.
The grey body of the tooth under the enamel.
Central dark canal, tapering to the apex.
Uniform thin line, a sign of health here.
The only real finding on this film.

cases.title: Bitewing, posterior caries
cases.description: Posterior bitewing. The bitewing is the workhorse for interproximal caries and crestal bone.
keyFindings: Interproximal caries on an upper premolar/molar
Interproximal caries on a lower molar
Alveolar crests are at a normal height

annotations.note: Where the upper and lower crowns meet. Caries hides just below the contacts.
Crestal bone level. Loss flags periodontal breakdown.
Compare height across the arch.
Radiolucent wedge on the distal contact.
A second lesion on the lower arch.
